answer:
This module is a self-paced professional development training designed to introduce pre-licensure nursing faculty to AI-assisted multiple-choice question development. By working through the various components of this module, you will explore how large language models can support the expert judgment required to write high-quality nursing MCQs.

The hands-on component guides you through a tool that includes pre-engineered prompts for ChatGPT, Gemini, and Copilot. By the end of the session, after you have been oriented to how it works, you will have the opportunity to use the tool yourself in a guided practice mode.

After the module, the tool will be available for you to use, adapt, and develop in whatever ways best suit your teaching needs.

question: Can I complete the training on my mobile device?
answer: Yes, the training module is usable on a wide range of devices. If at any point you would like to switch from one device to another, use the **Save and exit** to save your progress before opening the page on the device you are switching to.

question: Is a desktop or laptop recommended at any point?
answer:
When you reach the **hands-on activity**, you will be using the question-generation tool yourself, and you will need to switch views between the tool and your AI product. I anticipate that *most* people will prefer to use the tool this way, as it offers the convenience of a keyboard and a larger screen.

However, both this training module and the question-generation tool are fully compatible with mobile devices, and if you plan to primarily use the question-generation tool on your mobile device, it might be best to stay on it.

question: What data does the module collect?
answer:
The module records:
-- timestamps for when you began and ended the session, as well as your time on each individual card
-- whether you answered knowledge check questions correctly
-- your device's operating system and the width of its viewport

All of this data is assigned to your **participant code**.

question: Who can see my data?
answer: Only the researcher, Taemin Jin, has access to the data. The data will be used solely for dissertation research purposes and will either be reported only in aggregate form or appropriately deidentified.

question: Can I keep using the MCQ tool after the study is over?
answer: You are free to use the question-generation tool that you will learn in this training. Be sure to hold on to your **participant code**, as you will need it to access the tool.

question: Can I modify the prompts or adapt the tool for my courses?
answer: Yes, and you are encouraged to do so. The pre-designed prompts are intended as starting points rather than strict guidelines. You can adjust the wording, add context relevant to your clinical specialty, change the difficulty level, or rework the structure entirely to fit the types of questions your students need. The tool is designed to serve as a scaffold, not a script.

question: How much should I use it going forward?
answer:
Use the tool as much or as little as makes sense for your workflow. A few sample use cases:
-- Quickly draft practice questions with a lower bar for complexity, to give students enough material for meaningful practice
-- Draft a few higher-complexity questions to use in an in-class discussion activity
-- Use it after a class session to give students an opportunity to test their knowledge on what they just learned
-- Use it at the beginning of class to gauge baseline knowledge

There is no expectation for a specific frequency of use; use it in whatever way best supports your teaching.
